evals.description: Create a CI/CD pipeline for a Go project with devbox.json and Justfile, targeting GitHub Actions
evals.input: Set up CI/CD for this Go project. It uses devbox.json for tooling and Justfile for build commands. The repo is on GitHub.
evals.expected_behavior: Should detect Go build system, devbox.json tooling, recommend GitHub Actions, create incremental build filters, set up pre-built CI image if needed, add security scans, and propose guardrails interactively
pass_criteria: Detects Go build system and devbox.json
Recommends GitHub Actions as CI provider
Creates or proposes path-filter-based incremental builds
Adds or proposes security scans (at minimum secret + dependency scanning)
Checks devbox.json for missing CI tools (act, trivy, etc.)
Does not change build process without confirmation

evals.description: Audit an existing GitHub Actions pipeline that lacks incremental builds and security scans
evals.input: Audit and improve the CI/CD pipeline in this repo. It has GitHub Actions but builds everything every time and has no security scanning.
evals.expected_behavior: Should run analyze_pipeline.py, identify missing incremental builds and security scans, classify changes as automatic vs interactive, propose improvements with before/after
pass_criteria: Runs analyze_pipeline.py or equivalent detection
Identifies missing incremental builds (Critical/Important)
Identifies missing security scans (Critical/Important)
Classifies adding non-blocking security scans as automatic
Classifies adding blocking gates as interactive
Presents prioritized changes with before/after
Does not apply interactive changes without confirmation

evals.description: Set up a pre-built CI image for a project with slow Nix/devbox installs in CI
evals.input: CI takes 15 minutes because it installs Nix and devbox packages every run. The build itself is 30 seconds. Fix this.
evals.expected_behavior: Should identify the toolchain install as the bottleneck, propose a pre-built CI image published to GHCR, create a rebuild-trigger workflow, and modify the main workflow to use the image
pass_criteria: Identifies Nix/devbox install as the bottleneck
Proposes pre-built CI image with Dockerfile.ci
Creates rebuild-trigger workflow (on Dockerfile.ci/devbox.json/Justfile change)
Publishes to GHCR using GITHUB_TOKEN
Modifies main workflow to use the pre-built image
Removes or makes devbox-install-action conditional

evals.description: Fix tooling version misalignment between devbox.json, CI, and Dockerfile
evals.input: The devbox.json has Go 1.22, but the CI workflow installs Go 1.21 and the Dockerfile uses golang:1.20. Align these.
evals.expected_behavior: Should identify the version mismatch, propose devbox.json as single source of truth, update CI and Dockerfile to match, add alignment check
pass_criteria: Identifies Go version mismatch across devbox.json, CI, Dockerfile
Proposes devbox.json as the single source of truth
Updates CI workflow to use Go 1.22
Updates Dockerfile to use golang:1.22
Classifies as automatic (version alignment within compatible range)

evals.description: Distinguish between automatic and interactive CI changes when improving a pipeline
evals.input: Improve this pipeline. Add caching, add a blocking security gate, optimize the build, and change the deployment from push-to-main to blue-green.
evals.expected_behavior: Should classify caching and build optimization as automatic, blocking security gate and deployment strategy change as interactive, and handle each appropriately
pass_criteria: Classifies caching optimization as automatic
Classifies build optimization as automatic
Classifies blocking security gate as interactive (requires confirmation)
Classifies deployment strategy change as interactive (requires confirmation)
Applies automatic changes directly
Proposes interactive changes with before/after and waits for confirmation
